query: Iran IAEA cooperation law suspension 2025 2026, current state of Iran nuclear inspections and diplomacy, US Iran nuclear deal negotiations status June July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of what I found:

**The situation has been fundamentally reshaped by the 2026 Iran war and a war-ending peace framework.** Key findings:

1. **The IAEA cooperation suspension law** (passed by Iran's parliament in late June 2025 after the June 2025 strikes) remains in force. It requires Supreme National Security Council (SNSC) approval for any IAEA inspections. A partial cooperation framework was restored via the September 2025 Cairo agreement.

2. **A 2026 Iran war** began Feb 28, 2026 (US/Israeli strikes), reportedly including the assassinations of Supreme Leader Khamenei and negotiator Larijani, followed by ceasefires in April.

3. **The Islamabad Memorandum** (14-point framework) was signed June 17, 2026 by Trump and Pezeshkian, brokered by Pakistan. It ends the war, reopens the Strait of Hormuz, waives oil sanctions for 60 days, and establishes a **60-day window (to ~mid-August 2026)** for technical talks toward a final nuclear deal. Crucially, nuclear stockpile, missile, and proxy issues were all deferred.

4. **Current inspections dispute (late June/early July 2026):** IAEA chief Grossi and the US assert the MoU provides for inspector access to bombed enrichment sites; Iran insists inspections will only come after a final deal, with cooperation continuing under existing parliamentary law and SNSC decisions. No final deal had been concluded as of early July 2026.

Note: I was unable to find sources dated in July 2026 itself — the most recent concrete reporting is from June 22–26, 2026. AskNews tools were unavailable (credit limit reached), so findings rely on web search results from Al Jazeera, NPR, CNBC, CNN, Press TV, and Wikipedia, cross-validated across sources.

key_facts: Iran's late-June 2025 parliamentary law requires Supreme National Security Council (SNSC) approval for any IAEA inspections of nuclear sites; it remains in force as of July 2026.
A 2026 Iran war (began Feb 28, 2026, with US/Israeli strikes) reportedly included the assassinations of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and negotiator Ali Larijani.
The Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding (a 14-point war-ending framework) was signed June 17, 2026 by Trump and President Pezeshkian, brokered mainly by Pakistan.
The MoU established a 60-day negotiating window (roughly June 20 to mid-August 2026) to reach a final nuclear deal; nuclear stockpile, missile, and proxy issues were deferred.
The MoU immediately waived (but did not eliminate) US sanctions on Iranian oil for 60 days, effective June 21, 2026, and reopens the Strait of Hormuz toll-free for 60 days.
Contingent on a final deal, the MoU envisions a $300 billion+ private investment fund for Iran, release of frozen assets, and downgrading Iranian uranium from weapons-grade to reactor-grade.
First Switzerland talks (Bürgenstock) were postponed June 19; the Lake Lucerne Summit June 21-22 produced a roadmap to a final deal within 60 days.
As of late June/early July 2026, IAEA inspector access to bombed enrichment sites is disputed and not operational: Grossi/US say access is agreed; Iran says inspections only after a final deal.
IAEA chief Grossi (June 24 & 26, 2026) said the MoU 'explicitly' provides for IAEA-supervised inspections and initial site-visit talks were held.
The IAEA Board of Governors adopted a resolution on June 10, 2026 (GOV/2026/40) urging Iran to cooperate with the Agency.
IAEA's Feb 27, 2026 report (GOV/2026/8) said most of Iran's highly enriched uranium was stored in an underground tunnel complex at Isfahan.
Iran FM spokesman Baghaei (June 22-24, 2026) said cooperation continues under existing parliamentary law and SNSC decisions, with 'no clear schedule' for inspections.

Toll-free period for Strait of Hormuz commercial shipping under the MoU
Proposed private investment fund for Iran contingent on a final deal
IAEA Board of Governors resolution urging Iran to cooperate (GOV/2026/40)
